channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Former Olympian pleads not guilty in reflecting pool vandalism charges
item.description: Canoeist David Hearn plead not guilty in D.C. Superior Court Thursday to a charge of destruction of property causing more than $1,000 in damage to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.
item.content:encoded:
Canoeist David Hearn plead not guilty in D.C. Superior Court Thursday to a charge of destruction of property causing more than $1,000 in damage to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.

(Image credit: Finn Gomez)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Former coach at Bucknell University charged in death of freshman football player
item.description: Former strength and conditioning coach Mark Kulbis was charged Monday in the death of Calvin "CJ" Dickey Jr., a freshman athlete with sickle-cell trait who collapsed during training in July 2024.
item.content:encoded: Former strength and conditioning coach Mark Kulbis was charged Monday in the death of Calvin "CJ" Dickey Jr., a freshman athlete with sickle-cell trait who collapsed during training in July 2024.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Graham Platner ends Senate bid. And, why Nolan Wells' death captured national attention
item.description: Democrat Graham Platner ended his bid last night for U.S. Senate. And, prominent civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been enlisted to help get answers in the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.
item.content:encoded:
Democrat Graham Platner ended his bid last night for U.S. Senate. And, prominent civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been enlisted to help get answers in the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.

(Image credit: Laura Brett)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of ballad 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' has died at 75
item.description: Tyler died "unexpectedly" in a hospital in Portugal where she was being treated for an illness, her family said Thursday in a statement on her website.
item.content:encoded:
Tyler died "unexpectedly" in a hospital in Portugal where she was being treated for an illness, her family said Thursday in a statement on her website.

(Image credit: Alastair Grant)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A Florida airport is officially renamed for Trump. What does he stand to gain?
item.description: Trump is the first president to have an airport named after him while in office. The Trump Organization says he won't get royalties from the renaming, but legal experts see potential loopholes.
item.content:encoded:
Trump is the first president to have an airport named after him while in office. The Trump Organization says he won't get royalties from the renaming, but legal experts see potential loopholes.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: How England's class divide shaped Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s likely next prime minister
item.description: As mayor of Manchester, Andy Burnham brought growth to the postindustrial city. Can he scale that nationally as the next prime minister?
item.content:encoded:
As mayor of Manchester, Andy Burnham brought growth to the postindustrial city. Can he scale that nationally as the next prime minister?

(Image credit: Jon Super)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: New aviation mechanics graduate with jobs in hand, thanks to a labor shortage
item.description: Aviation is literally soaring in the U.S., with record passenger numbers. But with a generation of mechanics set to leave the workforce, the industry needs new graduates to fill the gap.
item.content:encoded:
Aviation is literally soaring in the U.S., with record passenger numbers. But with a generation of mechanics set to leave the workforce, the industry needs new graduates to fill the gap.

(Image credit: Angus Mordant)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Campaign staffers keep trying to bet on races despite push to curb insider trading
item.description: Kalshi says it has blocked "dozens" of trades from campaign insiders, but experts say the company's approach leaves lots of potential loopholes. NPR has found at least one trade that slipped through.
item.content:encoded:
Kalshi says it has blocked "dozens" of trades from campaign insiders, but experts say the company's approach leaves lots of potential loopholes. NPR has found at least one trade that slipped through.

(Image credit: Allison Robbert)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: The Iran war has pushed some countries away from oil and toward clean energy
item.description: The new round of bombing in the Middle East has underscored the precarity of relying on fossil fuels. The war is speeding up the global transition to EVs, solar and batteries, experts say.
item.content:encoded:
The new round of bombing in the Middle East has underscored the precarity of relying on fossil fuels. The war is speeding up the global transition to EVs, solar and batteries, experts say.

(Image credit: Ezra Acayan)
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump criticizes Iran's leaders and says ceasefire is over, Middle East countries prepare for the potential of more war as U.S. and Iran renew strikes, Graham Platner drops his bid for Senate.
item.content:encoded: Trump criticizes Iran's leaders and says ceasefire is over, Middle East countries prepare for the potential of more war as U.S. and Iran renew strikes, Graham Platner drops his bid for Senate.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
